referenceProvenance: downstream Windows onscreen-client WinUI probe screenshots (private QA evidence)
evidenceBoundary: Real Windows reference PNGs, macOS runtime PNGs, and pixel-diff PNGs/JSON stay in the private QA evidence location and are never committed to the runtime repository. This manifest carries only sanitized scenario names and metrics.
maxChannelDeltaPolicy: maxChannelDelta stays diagnostic at 255; one high-contrast glyph edge can hit 255 even when the route is visually close.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Login route and credential form; route selection warning is zero.
mainVisualGaps: Centered login column, native field chrome, check box, and progress width now satisfy the focused route gate.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Status/loading/error states; route selection warning is zero.
mainVisualGaps: Status InfoBar and progress surfaces now satisfy the focused route L4 gate; RMSE remains above the focused L5 bar.

mainVisualGaps: Messages list/detail and multiline input now satisfy the broad route L4 gate.

mainVisualGaps: Home shell/list/detail surfaces now satisfy the broad route L4 gate.

mainVisualGaps: Admin dashboard status/list surfaces now satisfy the broad route L4 gate.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Admin workbench list/detail route; route selection warning is zero.
mainVisualGaps: Workbench list/detail and command area now satisfy broad L5 metrics.

mainVisualGaps: Command/search frame now satisfies the focused route L4 gate; RMSE remains above focused L5.

sharedGaps.gap: All eight private downstream route screenshots pass the required native comparison ladder with PNG Windows references.
sharedGaps.evidence: production-ready-final summary and per-scenario pixel-diff JSON.

sharedGaps.gap: Private downstream route success does not promote public component nativeQualityGrade rows by itself.
sharedGaps.evidence: component-quality-dashboard, native-quality-family-tranches, and release-candidate checks remain separate.

sharedGaps.gap: Some icons remain renderer primitives rather than literal native glyph rendering; this is not promoted as public native component quality.
sharedGaps.evidence: Manual screenshot review and renderer implementation notes.

thresholdLadder.purpose: Baseline: current failing evidence is complete and reviewable.
Coarse route alignment: remove top-left/full-width/page-frame drift.
thresholdLadder.manualGate: Content appears in the same route regions; no missing major surface.

thresholdLadder.purpose: Layout and density parity: align page columns, list/detail widths, row heights, and form natural sizing.
Control-family parity: match core chrome for forms, buttons, list rows, status, progress, and navigation.
thresholdLadder.manualGate: Native-visible chrome differences are minor, not structural.

thresholdLadder.purpose: Conservative native-comparison pass: pass the existing downstream comparison contract.
Premium production promotion: promote only after public component crops and private route screenshots agree.
thresholdLadder.changedPixels: <= 35% for app routes; <= 24% for focused command/status/form routes when achievable
thresholdLadder.manualGate: Manual review signs off no route/content mismatch, no missing affordance, no misleading status, no text overlap.

manualInspectionCriteria: Wrong route content or wrong selected navigation state versus native reference.
Missing user-visible control, status, command, close affordance, glyph, or label.
Text overlap, clipped critical text, leaked password content, or unreadable line height.
Control chrome communicates a different state than Windows, especially selected, focused, disabled, error, warning, success, loading, or checked.
Screenshot was generated without real Windows PNG provenance or with non-PNG replacement evidence.
Improvement comes only from loosening thresholds or hiding unsupported diagnostics.
